title: Prompting Foundations
description: Learn how to evaluate prompts, compare alternatives, and choose the best prompt for a specific goal.

lessons.title: What You'll Learn
lessons.summary: See how this course will help you recognize, compare, and choose better prompts.

steps.text: Prompts guide AI behavior. Small wording changes can produce very different results.
In this course, you'll learn to recognize what a prompt is, discriminate between stronger and weaker prompts, and choose the best prompt for a specific goal.

steps.question: What skill will you develop in this course?
options: Evaluating which prompt best fits a specific goal
steps.explanation: This course focuses on choosing and improving prompts based on the result you want.

steps.question: Which activity best reflects this skill?
options: Choosing between multiple prompts based on the desired result
steps.explanation: The goal is to judge which prompt fits the task best, not just to make prompts longer.

steps.title: Start
steps.text: Let's begin with the basic idea of a prompt.

lessons.title: What is a Prompt?
lessons.summary: Learn what a prompt is and how it influences the structure and usefulness of AI output.

steps.text: A prompt is the input you give an AI system to guide its response.
A prompt influences what is answered, how it is structured, and how useful the result will be.

steps.question: Which best describes a prompt?
options: A dataset used to train AI
The input used to guide an AI response
A stored output from a previous interaction
steps.explanation: A prompt is the input that guides the AI's output.

steps.question: Which prompt most strongly influences how the answer is structured?
options: Provide a step-by-step explanation of machine learning
Explain why machine learning is important
steps.explanation: Asking for a step-by-step explanation directly influences the structure of the response.

steps.title: Next Lesson
steps.text: Now let's look at clarity and why mixed-intent prompts often fail.

lessons.title: Clarity: Single Intent
lessons.summary: See why single-intent prompts are easier for AI to answer clearly and usefully.

steps.text: Clear prompts focus on one defined outcome. Unclear prompts often mix multiple goals or unrelated questions.
When a prompt asks for explanation, judgment, and advice all at once, the AI may produce a response that feels scattered or confusing.
Some prompts look reasonable at first glance but still mix too many intentions. We'll explore this more deeply later.

steps.question: Which prompt is the clearest?
options: Explain business risk and how to reduce it and whether it is good or bad
Explain three types of business risk
Explain three types of business risk and how to reduce each
steps.explanation: This prompt has a clear task and a clear scope without mixing in unrelated judgments.

steps.question: Which prompt is most likely to produce a confusing response?
options: Explain inflation and how to respond to it personally and whether it is good or bad
steps.explanation: This prompt combines explanation, personal advice, and judgment in one request.

steps.question: Goal: You want actionable advice. Which prompt is best?
options: Explain three types of business risk and how to reduce each
steps.explanation: If the goal is action, the prompt should include both the risks and what to do about them.

steps.title: Next Lesson
steps.text: Next, we'll look at specificity and why targeted prompts usually produce more useful answers.

lessons.title: Specificity: Broad vs Targeted
lessons.summary: Learn how added specificity reduces guesswork and produces more actionable answers.

steps.text: Specific prompts reduce ambiguity and guide the AI toward a more useful response.
Broad prompts may still work, but they often produce generic answers because the AI has to guess what level, domain, or use case you really want.

steps.question: Which prompt is most specific?
options: Explain how AI is used in retail inventory management
steps.explanation: This prompt narrows the topic to a specific business use case.

steps.question: Which prompt is too broad to be highly useful?
options: Explain how to write a Python loop
Explain how to write a Python loop for processing orders
steps.explanation: 'Explain Python' is broad enough that the response could go in many directions.

steps.question: Goal: You want a practical coding example. Which prompt is best?
options: Show how to write a Python loop that processes a list of customer orders
steps.explanation: The best prompt matches the practical goal with a concrete use case.

steps.title: Next Lesson
steps.text: Now let's see how constraints can help—or hurt—depending on how they are used.

lessons.title: Constraints: Helpful vs Harmful
lessons.summary: Understand how constraints can improve clarity—or become so restrictive that they reduce usefulness.

steps.text: Constraints can improve clarity by limiting length, scope, or structure.
Helpful constraints guide the answer. Harmful constraints force oversimplified or low-information results.
Later, we'll go deeper into prompts that force overly simple answers and why that matters.

steps.question: Which prompt uses helpful constraints?
options: List three causes of climate change
Explain climate change in one sentence
steps.explanation: This prompt narrows the answer in a useful way without making it meaningless.

steps.question: Which prompt is overly restrictive?
options: List three benefits of renewable energy
Explain renewable energy in one sentence
Explain how renewable energy impacts the economy
steps.explanation: A one-sentence limit is often too restrictive for a useful explanation.

steps.question: Goal: You want a quick but useful summary. Which prompt is best?
options: List three benefits of renewable energy
Explain renewable energy in one sentence
Explain renewable energy in full detail
steps.explanation: This prompt balances brevity and usefulness.

steps.title: Next Lesson
steps.text: Next, we'll look at how to control the structure of the answer itself.

lessons.title: Output Format: Control the Structure
lessons.summary: Use lists, steps, and comparisons to shape the structure of AI output.

steps.text: You can guide AI output by specifying a format such as a list, a sequence of steps, or a comparison.
Format control makes answers easier to use because it shapes the response before it is generated.

steps.question: Which prompt controls output format?
options: Provide a step-by-step plan for project planning
steps.explanation: Requesting a step-by-step plan directly controls the structure of the answer.

steps.question: Which prompt does NOT effectively control structure?
options: Provide a list of 5 planning steps
Explain planning in a structured format
steps.explanation: This prompt gives no structure at all.

steps.question: Goal: You want a comparison. Which prompt is best?
options: Compare project planning and execution in two sentences
steps.explanation: The prompt directly asks for the format and the goal: a quick comparison.

steps.title: Next Lesson
steps.text: Now we'll combine the earlier ideas and practice choosing the best overall prompt.

lessons.title: Combining Skills
lessons.summary: Practice combining clarity, specificity, constraints, and format to choose the strongest prompt overall.

steps.text: Good prompts often combine multiple strengths: they are clear, specific, appropriately constrained, and structured for the goal.
The best prompt is not always the shortest or the longest. It is the one that best fits the intended result.

steps.question: Which prompt combines multiple good practices?
options: List three ways to improve cardiovascular health
Provide a step-by-step 30-day plan to improve cardiovascular health
steps.explanation: This prompt is specific, structured, and action-oriented.

steps.question: Which prompt is weakest overall?
options: Explain renewable energy in one sentence
steps.explanation: This prompt is overly restrictive and likely to produce an undersized answer.

steps.question: Goal: Quick comparison. Which prompt is best?
options: Compare AI and machine learning in two sentences
steps.explanation: The prompt directly matches the goal and keeps the answer short and structured.

steps.title: Final Lesson
steps.text: Let's wrap up with the next step in improving your prompting skill.

lessons.title: What To Do Next
lessons.summary: See how refinement and prompt debugging build on this foundation.

steps.text: You now have a foundation for evaluating prompts. The next step is learning how to refine prompts based on weak or unexpected results.
Later courses will go deeper into prompt engineering, prompt debugging, misleading constraints, and more advanced edge cases.

steps.question: What improves prompting skill most?
steps.explanation: Prompting improves through testing, observing results, and refining structure and intent.

steps.question: What should you do if a prompt produces weak results?
options: Rewrite the prompt with better structure and intent
steps.explanation: Weak results are usually a cue to refine the prompt, not to give up on the task.

steps.title: Complete
steps.text: You've finished Prompting Foundations. Next, we'll go deeper into refinement, debugging, and stronger prompt design.
